summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The American Customer Satisfaction ETF (ACSI) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund tracks an index of tier-weighted US large-cap companies assigned with the highest customer satisfaction scores. Holdings are weighted equally within each industry. ACSI was launched on Nov 1, 2016 and is issued by ACSI Funds.

data.title: The American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI): Quarter 1, 2026 Customer Satisfaction Weakens: Pent-Up Defection Intensifies
data.text: ANN ARBOR, Mich.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #ACSI--Customer satisfaction in the U.S. fell 0.3% to 76.7 (100-point scale) in Q1 2026, according to the ACSI.

data.title: American Customer Satisfaction Index Reveals High Customer Satisfaction, Strong Economy in Q3 2024
data.text: ANN ARBOR, Mich.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #CustomerSatisfaction--Customer satisfaction in the U.S. stands at 77.9 in Q3 2024, nearly the highest it has ever been, according to the ACSI.

news.t: The American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI): Quarter 1, 2026 Customer Satisfaction Weakens: Pent-Up Defection Intensifies
American Customer Satisfaction Index Reveals High Customer Satisfaction, Strong Economy in Q3 2024

holdings.infoBox: ACSI is an equity ETF with a total of 33 individual holdings. The top holdings are Apple stock at 7.67%, Meta Platforms at 5.12%, Amazon.com at 4.73%, Alphabet at 4.48%, and Dell Technologies at 4.39%.
